# GitHub Copilot Repository Instructions

## Repository Purpose

This repository is a Business Analyst portfolio and documentation project.

It documents the business analysis process for the Shopping Cart module
of the Đại Phú silk flower e-commerce website.

The repository does not contain a working website or production software.

## Candidate Role

The candidate participated as:

- Business Analyst Intern
- Team Leader of a six-member student team

Always distinguish between:

- Individual responsibilities
- Team responsibilities
- Work reviewed or guided by the internship supervisor

## Strict Restrictions

- Do not generate application source code.
- Do not create HTML, CSS, JavaScript or TypeScript.
- Do not create React, Vue, Angular or frontend application files.
- Do not create backend source code.
- Do not create database source code.
- Do not create package.json.
- Do not create a functional website.
- Do not create deployment configuration.
- Do not create CI/CD workflows.
- Do not claim that the website was deployed.
- Do not claim that the system was released to production.
- Do not claim that complete UAT was performed.
- Do not invent business metrics.
- Do not invent conversion-rate improvements.
- Do not invent stakeholder feedback.
- Do not invent project results.

## Allowed File Types

Only create or update:

- Markdown documentation
- Mermaid diagrams inside Markdown files
- Repository README
- Issue templates
- Requirement tables
- Portfolio documentation

## Documentation Language

Write the main content in Vietnamese.

Retain important Business Analyst terms in English where useful, such as:

- Requirement Elicitation
- Requirement Analysis
- User Flow
- Use Case
- User Story
- Acceptance Criteria
- Business Rule
- Functional Requirement
- Non-functional Requirement
- Test Case
- Requirement Traceability Matrix

## Documentation Standards

- Use professional Business Analyst terminology.
- Use clear headings and tables.
- Give each requirement a unique ID.
- Make requirements measurable and testable.
- Separate requirements from business rules.
- Separate completed work from proposed improvements.
- Preserve consistency among User Stories, Acceptance Criteria and Test Cases.
- Mention project limitations clearly.
- Do not present proposed content as completed internship work.

## Source of Truth

Use the following files as the primary source of truth:

- source-documents/source-material.md
- source-documents/internship-report-redacted.pdf
- source-documents/internship-log-redacted.pdf

When information is missing or ambiguous:

- Mark it as "Needs confirmation".
- Do not make assumptions.
- Do not invent details.

## Required Repository Structure

Create documentation under the following folders:

- docs/
- diagrams/
- assets/screenshots/
- source-documents/
